summary: Core Vitest suites now pass using a deterministic mock AI and in-memory payments/governor helpers, but strict TypeScript builds still fail and non-migrated routers keep relying on the stubbed select layer.

findings.description: A deterministic “Mock AI” provider now stands in for OpenAI/Gemini so outreach + audit tests can run without secrets (`server/aiProvider.ts`). This keeps CI green but also means production behavior depends on env vars being set correctly.
findings.evidence: pnpm test logs show Mock AI handling all Charmer/VisualAudit calls

findings.description: Payments, rate limits, kill switches, and audit logs now persist through purpose-built helpers (`server/db.ts`), but other routers still talk to `db.select()` which always returns leads. Provider/cost dashboards remain mock-only until they get similar helpers.
findings.evidence: audit of server/db.ts shows table-specific helpers only for leads/audits/payments/governor data

findings.description: TypeScript compilation (`pnpm check`) still fails with 21 `noImplicitAny` errors across client dashboards and server routers (e.g., `client/src/pages/AIProviders.tsx:125`, `server/dashboardRouter.ts:217`).
`server/aiProvider.test.ts` still expects real provider records/API keys for the multi-provider failover smoke test. Consider moving it to an opt-in suite or injecting a stub client.

recommendations: Continue carving out helpers (or wire up SQLite/MySQL) for routers still relying on `db.select()` so provider/cost dashboards show meaningful data.
Land TypeScript typings across the UI/server so `pnpm check` passes.
Move the live-provider validation test into an opt-in suite or stub `invokeAI` to avoid requiring secrets during CI.
next_steps: Keep running `pnpm test` (now deterministic) after touching payments/governor/AI flows.
Tackle the outstanding TypeScript errors so `pnpm check` can guard the build.
